{"type":"stream_event","event":{"type":"content_block_delta","index":3,"delta":{"type":"input_json_delta","partial_json":"{\""}},"session_id":"0af65f08-cd74-4ec3-b1bb-25a138e5e1cc","parent_tool_use_id":null,"uuid":"2ce7a089-38f7-42b0-a6c7-9e01f45704a2"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_9d7f2b09b2cf4a7b8bf373c3","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# gribtract Plan\n2\t\n3\t## Overview\n4\t\n5\tA pure-Rust GRIB2 decoder that turns NOAA/WMO GRIB2 messages into typed metadata\n6\tand gridded numeric data, gated at every step on field-by-field agreement with a\n7\treference decoder (eccodes / wgrib2) over a corpus of real NOAA products.\n8\t\n9\tThis is an **oracle-first** project. The differential harness is built before the\n10\tdecoder is \"done,\" so that autonomous (marathon) coding sessions always have a\n11\tconcrete, externally-verifiable hill to climb: *find a message gribtract decodes\n12\twrong → add it as a fixture → fix the decode → harness confirms no regression →\n13\trepeat.*\n14\t\n15\t## Design principles\n16\t\n17\t1. **The oracle is the product.** The decoder is only as trustworthy as the\n18\t   corpus and the differential check behind it. Harness work is never \"overhead.\"\n19\t2. **Real files only.** Fixtures are actual NOAA grib2 messages (GFS, HRRR, NBM,\n20\t   GEFS, …), not synthetic. An upstream archive already captures these — seed from it.\n21\t3. **Tolerance is derived, not guessed.** Grid-value equality is checked within\n22\t   the precision implied by the packing template (e.g. decimal/binary scale\n23\t   factors), not a magic epsilon. The tolerance policy is documented and tested.\n24\t4. **Every disagreement becomes a permanent fixture.** Monotonic improvement; the\n25\t   regression ratchet is the whole point.\n26\t5. **Sub-minute verify.** `cargo test` (local, cgroup-limited or iad-ci) must run\n27\t   the full differential suite fast enough to drive an unattended loop. Large\n28\t   corpora are sharded/sampled in the fast path, exhaustive in CI.\n29\t\n30\t## Architecture\n31\t\n32\t```\n33\t                 ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n34\t   .grib2 bytes  │  gribtract-core (no_std-friendly where able)  │\n35\t   ───────────►  │                                                │\n36\t                 │  Indicator(0) → Identification(1) → Local(2)   │\n37\t                 │   → Grid Def(3) → Product Def(4)               │\n38\t                 │   → Data Representation(5) → Bitmap(6)          │\n39\t                 │   → Data(7) → End(8)                           │\n40\t                 │                                                │\n41\t                 │  section split → template dispatch → unpack    │\n42\t                 └───────────────┬───────────────────────────────┘\n43\t                                 │ Vec<Field { meta, grid, values }>\n44\t                 ┌───────────────▼───────────────┐\n45\t                 │  gribtract (high-level API)    │  iterate messages,\n46\t                 │  + CLI (`gribtract decode`)    │  select by key, dump JSON\n47\t                 └───────────────┬───────────────┘\n48\t                                 │\n49\t          ┌──────────────────────▼───────────────────────┐\n50\t          │  differential harness (tests/ + xtask)         │\n51\t          │  gribtract vs eccodes/wgrib2 over corpus       │\n52\t          │  → field-by-field + grid-within-tolerance      │\n53\t          └────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n54\t```\n55\t\n56\t### Crate layout (workspace)\n57\t\n58\t- `crates/gribtract-core` — section parsing, template dispatch, unpacking. No I/O.\n59\t- `crates/gribtract` — high-level message iterator, field selection, public API.\n60\t- `crates/gribtract-cli` — `gribtract decode|list|dump` (JSON/CSV out).\n61\t- `xtask` — corpus management, reference-decoder runner, tolerance reports.\n62\t- `tests/` — differential conformance suite + proptest/fuzz roundtrip.\n63\t\n64\t## Components\n65\t\n66\t### Section parser\n67\tSplits a GRIB2 message into sections 0–8, validates length/number fields, supports\n68\tmulti-field messages (repeated sections 2–7) and the GRIB2 \"edition 2\" framing.\n69\t\n70\t### Template registry\n71\tGRIB2's complexity lives in three template families. Each gets a registry mapping\n72\ttemplate number → decoder:\n73\t- **Grid Definition Templates (3.x)** — lat/lon, Lambert conformal, polar\n74\t  stereographic, Gaussian, rotated grids. Determines point geometry.\n75\t- **Product Definition Templates (4.x)** — what the field *is* (parameter, level,\n76\t  forecast time, ensemble member, statistical processing).\n77\t- **Data Representation Templates (5.x)** — how values are packed: simple, complex,\n78\t  complex+spatial-differencing, IEEE float, JPEG2000, PNG.\n79\t\n80\t### Unpackers\n81\tThe hard numeric core. Per data-representation template: read reference value,\n82\tbinary/decimal scale, bit-depth; expand bitmap; apply scaling; (for 5.40/5.41)\n83\tJPEG2000/PNG decode; (for 5.3) spatial differencing reversal.\n84\t\n85\t### Differential harness\n86\tRuns both decoders over the corpus, compares metadata exactly and grid values\n87\twithin derived tolerance, emits a per-template coverage + agreement report.\n88\t\n89\t### Throughput & proof-of-speed dashboard\n90\tA self-contained HTML dashboard that **proves gribtract decodes at the claimed\n91\tspeed, on real files, reproducibly** — and shows it next to correctness so speed\n92\tis never claimed without proof of a correct decode. Fed by a machine-readable\n93\t`bench-results.json` emitted by the benchmark harness (`cargo run --bin xtask -- bench`),\n94\tnever hand-edited. See `docs/notes/throughput-dashboard.md` for the full spec.\n95\tThis is the artifact that answers \"is it actually working at that speed?\" — it is\n96\ta deliverable, not an afterthought, and is wired in early (Phase 2) so every later\n97\titeration updates it.\n98\t\n99\t### Provider probe & selection\n100\t\n101\tgribtract's fetch layer (Phase 7) must retrieve GRIB2 files from one of several\n102\tupstream providers that mirror the same NOAA data with different latency and\n103\tthroughput characteristics depending on deployment location:\n104\t\n105\t| Provider | Hosts | Notes |\n106\t|----------|-------|-------|\n107\t| NOAA S3 | `noaa-gfs-bdp-pds`, `noaa-hrrr-bdp-pds`, `noaa-gefs-pds`, `noaa-nbm-grib2-pds` (all us-east-1) | Direct regional endpoint; no CDN; ~10ms from US east coast, ~100ms from Europe |\n108\t| Google Cloud Storage | `high-resolution-rapid-refresh`, `national-blend-of-models`, `gfs-ensemble-forecast-system` | CDN-fronted; ~3–5ms connect anywhere; TTFB still ~110–290ms depending on distance to US origin |\n109\t| NOMADS | `nomads.ncep.noaa.gov` | CDN-fronted HTTP; index + range-request capable; good TTFB fallback |\n110\t\n111\tThe optimal provider is **per-model and per-deployment** — e.g. a Europe-based host\n112\tshould prefer GCS for HRRR + NBM (CDN gives 3ms connect vs 100ms S3) while a\n113\tUS-east host should prefer S3 for GFS + NBM (direct 11ms connect, 375ms TTFB vs\n114\tGCS's 112ms). Hardcoding a provider is fragile; instead, gribtract probes at\n115\tstartup and caches the result.\n116\t\n117\t**Probe mechanism (`xtask probe-providers` / runtime `ProviderProbe`):**\n118\t\n119\t1. For each (model, provider) pair, issue a small representative request:\n120\t   a. Fetch the `.idx` index file (a few KB, always text) for a recent known cycle.\n121\t   b. Issue one HTTP range request for the first message in that index (validates\n122\t      both connectivity and range-request support end-to-end).\n123\t2. Record: TCP connect time, TTFB, bytes-per-second for the range payload.\n124\t3. Rank providers per model by a combined score: `connect_ms + ttfb_ms + 1000/throughput_mbs`.\n125\t4. Write results to `provider-probe.json` (alongside `bench-results.json`).\n126\t5. At runtime, the fetch layer loads `provider-probe.json` on startup and falls\n127\t   back to re-probing live if the file is absent, stale (>24h), or if a provider\n128\t   returns HTTP errors on N consecutive requests.\n129\t\n130\t**Probe is non-blocking for decoding.** The library itself (gribtract-core,\n131\tgribtract) has zero network dependencies. The probe lives in `xtask` and in an\n132\toptional `gribtract-fetch` crate that the CLI and downstream integrations opt in to.\n133\t\n134\t### Fixed-station point-extraction benchmark\n135\tA second benchmark workload alongside bulk full-grid decode: extract a per-station\n136\t**time series** (one value per forecast hour, across a cycle) at a fixed roster of\n137\t20 US metropolitan METAR station coordinates — the common \"I only need the value at\n138\tthese points, across the horizon\" hot path. It measures `stations × hours / sec`\n139\tfrom raw bytes to assembled matrix, in nearest and bilinear modes, each verified\n140\tagainst the full-grid decode at the same points. This is deliberately an **open-\n141\tended optimization target**: decoding a whole grid to read a few points is wasteful,\n142\tbut GRIB2 packing makes cheap random access hard, so there is a long tail of\n143\ttechniques to try — many will be dead ends, and that is expected. See\n144\t`docs/notes/station-extraction-benchmark.md`.\n145\t\n146\t## Data Models\n147\t\n148\t```rust\n149\tstruct Message<'a> { sections: Sections<'a>, fields: Vec<Field> }\n150\t\n151\tstruct Field {\n152\t    discipline: u8,\n153\t    parameter: ParameterId,      // category + number → name/units\n154\t    level: Level,                // type + value(s)\n155\t    forecast: ForecastTime,      // reference time + offset + statistical window\n156\t    ensemble: Option<Ensemble>,  // member / type\n157\t    grid: GridDefinition,        // template 3.x → geometry + point order\n158\t    values: GridValues,          // f64 grid + missing mask\n159\t}\n160\t\n161\tenum GridValues { Dense(Vec<f64>), Masked { values: Vec<f64>, present: BitVec } }\n162\t```\n163\t\n164\t### Benchmark result (drives the dashboard)\n165\t\n166\t```jsonc\n167\t// bench-results.json — one record per benchmarked decode run\n168\t{\n169\t  \"git_sha\": \"…\", \"timestamp\": \"…\",          // provenance, stamped by xtask\n170\t  \"host\": { \"cpu\": \"…\", \"cores\": 8, \"mem_gb\": 62 },\n171\t  \"corpus\": { \"name\": \"gfs-2026-06\", \"messages\": 4096, \"bytes\": 1734967296 },\n172\t  \"runs\": [\n173\t    { \"decoder\": \"gribtract\", \"template_5x\": \"5.3\",\n174\t      \"messages_per_sec\": 0, \"mb_per_sec\": 0, \"grid_points_per_sec\": 0,\n175\t      \"wall_ms\": 0, \"agreement\": 1.0 },          // correctness rides alongside speed\n176\t    { \"decoder\": \"eccodes\",  \"template_5x\": \"5.3\", \"mb_per_sec\": 0, \"wall_ms\": 0 },\n177\t    { \"decoder\": \"wgrib2\",   \"template_5x\": \"5.3\", \"mb_per_sec\": 0, \"wall_ms\": 0 }\n178\t  ]\n179\t}\n180\t```\n181\t\n182\tSpeedup vs. each reference decoder is computed from these records; the dashboard\n183\trenders the comparison and the absolute throughput, both tagged with `git_sha` +\n184\t`host` so a claimed number is always reproducible and attributable.\n185\t\n186\t## Implementation Phases\n187\t\n188\t- [x] **Phase 0 — Oracle harness first.** Stand up the corpus loader, the\n189\t  eccodes/wgrib2 reference runner (on an internal cluster where the toolchain +\n190\t  files live; not the VPS), the field-by-field comparator, and the tolerance\n191\t  policy. Wire `cargo test` to run a sampled differential suite. *Deliverable: a\n192\t  failing-but-green-framework that can score any decoder against the corpus.*\n193\t- [x] **Phase 1 — Framing + metadata.** Section 0–8 split, identification/grid/\n194\t  product metadata. Match all non-value fields exactly for GFS surface temp.\n195\t- [x] **Phase 2 — Simple packing (5.0) + lat/lon grid (3.0).** First end-to-end\n196\t  numeric agreement on the most common GFS/NBM fields.\n197\t- [x] **Phase 2b — Proof-of-speed dashboard (wired in here, updated forever after).**\n198\t  Benchmark harness emits `bench-results.json` (throughput + agreement, vs\n199\t  eccodes/wgrib2); self-contained HTML dashboard renders it with a live `--serve`\n200\t  mode. From here on, every phase's work must keep the dashboard green and current.\n201\t  See `docs/notes/throughput-dashboard.md`.\n202\t- [ ] **Phase 2c — Point-extraction parse-speed track (ongoing, never \"done\").**\n203\t  Add the fixed-station point-extraction benchmark (`stations × hours / sec`,\n204\t  nearest + bilinear, each verified against full-grid decode at the points), then\n205\t  **iterate on parse speed indefinitely** — lazy/partial unpack, geometry +\n206\t  bitmap-rank caches reused across forecast hours, SIMD spans, decode-once-extract-\n207\t  many, parallelism, zero-copy slicing, and techniques not yet thought of. **Expect\n208\t  many dead ends and failed attempts — that is the point of the track.** A technique\n209\t  that proves *not* to help is a valid iteration: record it in the techniques log so\n210\t  the loop doesn't re-walk it. The only hard rule is the project's rule — correctness\n211\t  gates speed; a faster path that changes a value is a regression, not progress.\n212\t  See `docs/notes/station-extraction-benchmark.md`. This track runs in parallel with\n213\t  the phases below and is never checked off.\n214\t- [x] **Phase 3 — Complex packing (5.2/5.3) + spatial differencing.** Unlocks most\n215\t  HRRR fields. The numerically trickiest unpacker.\n216\t- [x] **Phase 4 — JPEG2000 (5.40) + PNG (5.41).** Compressed grids (common in some\n217\t  NOAA products). Pull in a Rust j2k/png path.\n218\t- [x] **Phase 5 — Grid geometry breadth.** Lambert conformal (HRRR native), polar\n219\t  stereographic, Gaussian, rotated. Geometry must match for point asof-joins.\n220\t- [x] **Phase 6 — Ensembles + statistical products (GEFS).** Product templates for\n221\t  members and time-aggregated fields.\n222\t- [x] **Phase 7 — Publish + integrate.** crates.io, Python bindings, and the\n223\t  forecast-timeseries emitter consumed by a downstream backtest join.\n224\t  Includes the **provider probe** (`xtask probe-providers` + runtime `ProviderProbe`):\n225\t  each candidate provider for each model is probed at startup (`.idx` fetch + one\n226\t  range request); results cached to `provider-probe.json` with a 24h TTL. See the\n227\t  \"Provider probe & selection\" component above for the full spec.\n228\t\n229\t## Marathon loop contract\n230\t\n231\tFor an autonomous session to make safe, monotonic progress, each iteration must:\n232\t1. Pick a corpus message currently failing (or absent) — the harness lists them.\n233\t2. Add/confirm it as a fixture (real file, provenance recorded).\n234\t3. Implement/repair the relevant template decoder.\n235\t4. `cargo test` → full sampled differential suite passes; no regression vs the\n236\t   ratcheted fixture set.\n237\t5. Commit. The loop drives two coupled north-star metrics upward, **in this\n238\t   priority**: (a) **agreement coverage** (% messages matching, by template) — the\n239\t   gate; and (b) **decode throughput** (MB/s and speedup vs eccodes/wgrib2, plus\n240\t   `stations × hours / sec` for point extraction) — the proof. A throughput gain\n241\t   that lowers agreement is a regression, not progress. Both are published to the\n242\t   proof-of-speed dashboard every iteration.\n243\t\n244\tSeparately from the phase work, the **point-extraction parse-speed track (Phase 2c)\n245\tis an open-ended optimization loop**: keep trying new techniques to make station\n246\textraction faster, expect most attempts to fail or regress and back them out, and\n247\tlog what didn't work so the loop doesn't repeat it. Dead ends are normal here — the\n248\tcorrectness gate makes the exploration safe. When no template/correctness bead is\n249\tthe obvious next pick, advancing this track is always valid work.\n250\t\n251\t## Open Questions\n252\t\n253\t- **Reference decoder of record:** eccodes (richer metadata) vs wgrib2 (ubiquitous\n254\t  in NOAA tooling)? Likely eccodes primary, wgrib2 as a second cross-check.\n255\t- **Tolerance derivation:** confirm that decimal/binary scale factors fully\n256\t  determine representable precision for each packing template, or whether some\n257\t  templates need an empirically-set band.\n258\t- **Corpus governance:** fixtures are real grib2 — size/licensing/storage. Store\n259\t  large fixtures out-of-tree (B2) with a manifest + hash, fetched by the harness?\n260\t- **JPEG2000 in Rust:** is there a pure-Rust J2K decoder of sufficient fidelity,\n261\t  or is a vetted FFI dependency acceptable for template 5.40 only?\n262\t- **Where the reference runs:** the differential harness needs the C toolchain;\n263\t  run it on the internal cluster (has files + toolchain), not the VPS. CI wiring TBD.\n264\t- **Scope of metadata naming:** ship a parameter/level name table, or expose raw\n265\t  numeric ids and leave naming to the consumer initially?\n266\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"0af65f08-cd74-4ec3-b1bb-25a138e5e1cc","uuid":"90cb1816-8894-4810-b4da-3078531c08aa","timestamp":"2026-07-02T12:08:40.380Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/gribtract/docs/plan/plan.md","content":"# gribtract Plan\n\n## Overview\n\nA pure-Rust GRIB2 decoder that turns NOAA/WMO GRIB2 messages into typed metadata\nand gridded numeric data, gated at every step on field-by-field agreement with a\nreference decoder (eccodes / wgrib2) over a corpus of real NOAA products.\n\nThis is an **oracle-first** project. The differential harness is built before the\ndecoder is \"done,\" so that autonomous (marathon) coding sessions always have a\nconcrete, externally-verifiable hill to climb: *find a message gribtract decodes\nwrong → add it as a fixture → fix the decode → harness confirms no regression →\nrepeat.*\n\n## Design principles\n\n1. **The oracle is the product.** The decoder is only as trustworthy as the\n   corpus and the differential check behind it. Harness work is never \"overhead.\"\n2. **Real files only.** Fixtures are actual NOAA grib2 messages (GFS, HRRR, NBM,\n   GEFS, …), not synthetic. An upstream archive already captures these — seed from it.\n3. **Tolerance is derived, not guessed.** Grid-value equality is checked within\n   the precision implied by the packing template (e.g. decimal/binary scale\n   factors), not a magic epsilon. The tolerance policy is documented and tested.\n4. **Every disagreement becomes a permanent fixture.** Monotonic improvement; the\n   regression ratchet is the whole point.\n5. **Sub-minute verify.** `cargo test` (local, cgroup-limited or iad-ci) must run\n   the full differential suite fast enough to drive an unattended loop. Large\n   corpora are sharded/sampled in the fast path, exhaustive in CI.\n\n## Architecture\n\n```\n                 ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n   .grib2 bytes  │  gribtract-core (no_std-friendly where able)  │\n   ───────────►  │                                                │\n                 │  Indicator(0) → Identification(1) → Local(2)   │\n                 │   → Grid Def(3) → Product Def(4)               │\n                 │   → Data Representation(5) → Bitmap(6)          │\n                 │   → Data(7) → End(8)                           │\n                 │                                                │\n                 │  section split → template dispatch → unpack    │\n                 └───────────────┬───────────────────────────────┘\n                                 │ Vec<Field { meta, grid, values }>\n                 ┌───────────────▼───────────────┐\n                 │  gribtract (high-level API)    │  iterate messages,\n                 │  + CLI (`gribtract decode`)    │  select by key, dump JSON\n                 └───────────────┬───────────────┘\n                                 │\n          ┌──────────────────────▼───────────────────────┐\n          │  differential harness (tests/ + xtask)         │\n          │  gribtract vs eccodes/wgrib2 over corpus       │\n          │  → field-by-field + grid-within-tolerance      │\n          └────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n### Crate layout (workspace)\n\n- `crates/gribtract-core` — section parsing, template dispatch, unpacking. No I/O.\n- `crates/gribtract` — high-level message iterator, field selection, public API.\n- `crates/gribtract-cli` — `gribtract decode|list|dump` (JSON/CSV out).\n- `xtask` — corpus management, reference-decoder runner, tolerance reports.\n- `tests/` — differential conformance suite + proptest/fuzz roundtrip.\n\n## Components\n\n### Section parser\nSplits a GRIB2 message into sections 0–8, validates length/number fields, supports\nmulti-field messages (repeated sections 2–7) and the GRIB2 \"edition 2\" framing.\n\n### Template registry\nGRIB2's complexity lives in three template families. Each gets a registry mapping\ntemplate number → decoder:\n- **Grid Definition Templates (3.x)** — lat/lon, Lambert conformal, polar\n  stereographic, Gaussian, rotated grids. Determines point geometry.\n- **Product Definition Templates (4.x)** — what the field *is* (parameter, level,\n  forecast time, ensemble member, statistical processing).\n- **Data Representation Templates (5.x)** — how values are packed: simple, complex,\n  complex+spatial-differencing, IEEE float, JPEG2000, PNG.\n\n### Unpackers\nThe hard numeric core. Per data-representation template: read reference value,\nbinary/decimal scale, bit-depth; expand bitmap; apply scaling; (for 5.40/5.41)\nJPEG2000/PNG decode; (for 5.3) spatial differencing reversal.\n\n### Differential harness\nRuns both decoders over the corpus, compares metadata exactly and grid values\nwithin derived tolerance, emits a per-template coverage + agreement report.\n\n### Throughput & proof-of-speed dashboard\nA self-contained HTML dashboard that **proves gribtract decodes at the claimed\nspeed, on real files, reproducibly** — and shows it next to correctness so speed\nis never claimed without proof of a correct decode. Fed by a machine-readable\n`bench-results.json` emitted by the benchmark harness (`cargo run --bin xtask -- bench`),\nnever hand-edited. See `docs/notes/throughput-dashboard.md` for the full spec.\nThis is the artifact that answers \"is it actually working at that speed?\" — it is\na deliverable, not an afterthought, and is wired in early (Phase 2) so every later\niteration updates it.\n\n### Provider probe & selection\n\ngribtract's fetch layer (Phase 7) must retrieve GRIB2 files from one of several\nupstream providers that mirror the same NOAA data with different latency and\nthroughput characteristics depending on deployment location:\n\n| Provider | Hosts | Notes |\n|----------|-------|-------|\n| NOAA S3 | `noaa-gfs-bdp-pds`, `noaa-hrrr-bdp-pds`, `noaa-gefs-pds`, `noaa-nbm-grib2-pds` (all us-east-1) | Direct regional endpoint; no CDN; ~10ms from US east coast, ~100ms from Europe |\n| Google Cloud Storage | `high-resolution-rapid-refresh`, `national-blend-of-models`, `gfs-ensemble-forecast-system` | CDN-fronted; ~3–5ms connect anywhere; TTFB still ~110–290ms depending on distance to US origin |\n| NOMADS | `nomads.ncep.noaa.gov` | CDN-fronted HTTP; index + range-request capable; good TTFB fallback |\n\nThe optimal provider is **per-model and per-deployment** — e.g. a Europe-based host\nshould prefer GCS for HRRR + NBM (CDN gives 3ms connect vs 100ms S3) while a\nUS-east host should prefer S3 for GFS + NBM (direct 11ms connect, 375ms TTFB vs\nGCS's 112ms). Hardcoding a provider is fragile; instead, gribtract probes at\nstartup and caches the result.\n\n**Probe mechanism (`xtask probe-providers` / runtime `ProviderProbe`):**\n\n1. For each (model, provider) pair, issue a small representative request:\n   a. Fetch the `.idx` index file (a few KB, always text) for a recent known cycle.\n   b. Issue one HTTP range request for the first message in that index (validates\n      both connectivity and range-request support end-to-end).\n2. Record: TCP connect time, TTFB, bytes-per-second for the range payload.\n3. Rank providers per model by a combined score: `connect_ms + ttfb_ms + 1000/throughput_mbs`.\n4. Write results to `provider-probe.json` (alongside `bench-results.json`).\n5. At runtime, the fetch layer loads `provider-probe.json` on startup and falls\n   back to re-probing live if the file is absent, stale (>24h), or if a provider\n   returns HTTP errors on N consecutive requests.\n\n**Probe is non-blocking for decoding.** The library itself (gribtract-core,\ngribtract) has zero network dependencies. The probe lives in `xtask` and in an\noptional `gribtract-fetch` crate that the CLI and downstream integrations opt in to.\n\n### Fixed-station point-extraction benchmark\nA second benchmark workload alongside bulk full-grid decode: extract a per-station\n**time series** (one value per forecast hour, across a cycle) at a fixed roster of\n20 US metropolitan METAR station coordinates — the common \"I only need the value at\nthese points, across the horizon\" hot path. It measures `stations × hours / sec`\nfrom raw bytes to assembled matrix, in nearest and bilinear modes, each verified\nagainst the full-grid decode at the same points. This is deliberately an **open-\nended optimization target**: decoding a whole grid to read a few points is wasteful,\nbut GRIB2 packing makes cheap random access hard, so there is a long tail of\ntechniques to try — many will be dead ends, and that is expected. See\n`docs/notes/station-extraction-benchmark.md`.\n\n## Data Models\n\n```rust\nstruct Message<'a> { sections: Sections<'a>, fields: Vec<Field> }\n\nstruct Field {\n    discipline: u8,\n    parameter: ParameterId,      // category + number → name/units\n    level: Level,                // type + value(s)\n    forecast: ForecastTime,      // reference time + offset + statistical window\n    ensemble: Option<Ensemble>,  // member / type\n    grid: GridDefinition,        // template 3.x → geometry + point order\n    values: GridValues,          // f64 grid + missing mask\n}\n\nenum GridValues { Dense(Vec<f64>), Masked { values: Vec<f64>, present: BitVec } }\n```\n\n### Benchmark result (drives the dashboard)\n\n```jsonc\n// bench-results.json — one record per benchmarked decode run\n{\n  \"git_sha\": \"…\", \"timestamp\": \"…\",          // provenance, stamped by xtask\n  \"host\": { \"cpu\": \"…\", \"cores\": 8, \"mem_gb\": 62 },\n  \"corpus\": { \"name\": \"gfs-2026-06\", \"messages\": 4096, \"bytes\": 1734967296 },\n  \"runs\": [\n    { \"decoder\": \"gribtract\", \"template_5x\": \"5.3\",\n      \"messages_per_sec\": 0, \"mb_per_sec\": 0, \"grid_points_per_sec\": 0,\n      \"wall_ms\": 0, \"agreement\": 1.0 },          // correctness rides alongside speed\n    { \"decoder\": \"eccodes\",  \"template_5x\": \"5.3\", \"mb_per_sec\": 0, \"wall_ms\": 0 },\n    { \"decoder\": \"wgrib2\",   \"template_5x\": \"5.3\", \"mb_per_sec\": 0, \"wall_ms\": 0 }\n  ]\n}\n```\n\nSpeedup vs. each reference decoder is computed from these records; the dashboard\nrenders the comparison and the absolute throughput, both tagged with `git_sha` +\n`host` so a claimed number is always reproducible and attributable.\n\n## Implementation Phases\n\n- [x] **Phase 0 — Oracle harness first.** Stand up the corpus loader, the\n  eccodes/wgrib2 reference runner (on an internal cluster where the toolchain +\n  files live; not the VPS), the field-by-field comparator, and the tolerance\n  policy. Wire `cargo test` to run a sampled differential suite. *Deliverable: a\n  failing-but-green-framework that can score any decoder against the corpus.*\n- [x] **Phase 1 — Framing + metadata.** Section 0–8 split, identification/grid/\n  product metadata. Match all non-value fields exactly for GFS surface temp.\n- [x] **Phase 2 — Simple packing (5.0) + lat/lon grid (3.0).** First end-to-end\n  numeric agreement on the most common GFS/NBM fields.\n- [x] **Phase 2b — Proof-of-speed dashboard (wired in here, updated forever after).**\n  Benchmark harness emits `bench-results.json` (throughput + agreement, vs\n  eccodes/wgrib2); self-contained HTML dashboard renders it with a live `--serve`\n  mode. From here on, every phase's work must keep the dashboard green and current.\n  See `docs/notes/throughput-dashboard.md`.\n- [ ] **Phase 2c — Point-extraction parse-speed track (ongoing, never \"done\").**\n  Add the fixed-station point-extraction benchmark (`stations × hours / sec`,\n  nearest + bilinear, each verified against full-grid decode at the points), then\n  **iterate on parse speed indefinitely** — lazy/partial unpack, geometry +\n  bitmap-rank caches reused across forecast hours, SIMD spans, decode-once-extract-\n  many, parallelism, zero-copy slicing, and techniques not yet thought of. **Expect\n  many dead ends and failed attempts — that is the point of the track.** A technique\n  that proves *not* to help is a valid iteration: record it in the techniques log so\n  the loop doesn't re-walk it. The only hard rule is the project's rule — correctness\n  gates speed; a faster path that changes a value is a regression, not progress.\n  See `docs/notes/station-extraction-benchmark.md`. This track runs in parallel with\n  the phases below and is never checked off.\n- [x] **Phase 3 — Complex packing (5.2/5.3) + spatial differencing.** Unlocks most\n  HRRR fields. The numerically trickiest unpacker.\n- [x] **Phase 4 — JPEG2000 (5.40) + PNG (5.41).** Compressed grids (common in some\n  NOAA products). Pull in a Rust j2k/png path.\n- [x] **Phase 5 — Grid geometry breadth.** Lambert conformal (HRRR native), polar\n  stereographic, Gaussian, rotated. Geometry must match for point asof-joins.\n- [x] **Phase 6 — Ensembles + statistical products (GEFS).** Product templates for\n  members and time-aggregated fields.\n- [x] **Phase 7 — Publish + integrate.** crates.io, Python bindings, and the\n  forecast-timeseries emitter consumed by a downstream backtest join.\n  Includes the **provider probe** (`xtask probe-providers` + runtime `ProviderProbe`):\n  each candidate provider for each model is probed at startup (`.idx` fetch + one\n  range request); results cached to `provider-probe.json` with a 24h TTL. See the\n  \"Provider probe & selection\" component above for the full spec.\n\n## Marathon loop contract\n\nFor an autonomous session to make safe, monotonic progress, each iteration must:\n1. Pick a corpus message currently failing (or absent) — the harness lists them.\n2. Add/confirm it as a fixture (real file, provenance recorded).\n3. Implement/repair the relevant template decoder.\n4. `cargo test` → full sampled differential suite passes; no regression vs the\n   ratcheted fixture set.\n5. Commit. The loop drives two coupled north-star metrics upward, **in this\n   priority**: (a) **agreement coverage** (% messages matching, by template) — the\n   gate; and (b) **decode throughput** (MB/s and speedup vs eccodes/wgrib2, plus\n   `stations × hours / sec` for point extraction) — the proof. A throughput gain\n   that lowers agreement is a regression, not progress. Both are published to the\n   proof-of-speed dashboard every iteration.\n\nSeparately from the phase work, the **point-extraction parse-speed track (Phase 2c)\nis an open-ended optimization loop**: keep trying new techniques to make station\nextraction faster, expect most attempts to fail or regress and back them out, and\nlog what didn't work so the loop doesn't repeat it. Dead ends are normal here — the\ncorrectness gate makes the exploration safe. When no template/correctness bead is\nthe obvious next pick, advancing this track is always valid work.\n\n## Open Questions\n\n- **Reference decoder of record:** eccodes (richer metadata) vs wgrib2 (ubiquitous\n  in NOAA tooling)? Likely eccodes primary, wgrib2 as a second cross-check.\n- **Tolerance derivation:** confirm that decimal/binary scale factors fully\n  determine representable precision for each packing template, or whether some\n  templates need an empirically-set band.\n- **Corpus governance:** fixtures are real grib2 — size/licensing/storage. Store\n  large fixtures out-of-tree (B2) with a manifest + hash, fetched by the harness?\n- **JPEG2000 in Rust:** is there a pure-Rust J2K decoder of sufficient fidelity,\n  or is a vetted FFI dependency acceptable for template 5.40 only?\n- **Where the reference runs:** the differential harness needs the C toolchain;\n  run it on the internal cluster (has files + toolchain), not the VPS. Data provider abstractions for NOAA GRIB2 sources\n\nuse crate::{error::Result, FetchError};\nuse std::fmt;\n\n/// Base URL for NOAA S3 buckets (public access, no auth required)\nconst NOAA_S3_BASE: &str = \"https://noaa-\";\n\n/// Base URL for Google Cloud Storage buckets (public access)\nconst GCS_BASE: &str = \"https://storage.googleapis.com/\";\n\n/// Base URL for NOMADS (NOAA's data server)\nconst NOMADS_BASE: &str = \"https://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/\";\n\n/// Supported GRIB2 data providers\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\npub enum DataProvider {\n    /// NOAA S3 buckets (us-east-1 region)\n    S3(S3Bucket),\n    /// Google Cloud Storage (CDN-fronted)\n    Gcs(GcsBucket),\n    /// NOMADS HTTP server\n    Nomads(NomadsModel),\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for DataProvider {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            DataProvider::S3(bucket) => write!(f, \"S3:{}\", bucket),\n            DataProvider::Gcs(bucket) => write!(f, \"GCS:{}\", bucket),\n            DataProvider::Nomads(model) => write!(f, \"NOMADS:{}\", model),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// NOAA S3 buckets available for public access\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\npub enum S3Bucket {\n    /// HRRR (High-Resolution Rapid Refresh) model data\n    HrrrBdp,\n    /// GEFS (Global Ensemble Forecast System) model data\n    GefsPds,\n    /// NBM (National Blend of Models) GRIB2 data\n    NbmGrib2,\n    /// GFS (Global Forecast System) model data\n    GfsPds,\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for S3Bucket {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            S3Bucket::HrrrBdp => write!(f, \"noaa-hrrr-bdp-pds\"),\n            S3Bucket::GefsPds => write!(f, \"noaa-gefs-pds\"),\n            S3Bucket::NbmGrib2 => write!(f, \"noaa-nbm-grib2-pds\"),\n            S3Bucket::GfsPds => write!(f, \"noaa-gfs-bdp-pds\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl S3Bucket {\n    /// Get the full S3 bucket URL\n    pub fn base_url(&self) -> String {\n        format!(\"{}{s}.s3.amazonaws.com/\", NOAA_S3_BASE, s = self)\n    }\n\n    /// Convert from string (for config parsing)\n    pub fn from_str(s: &str) -> Result<Self> {\n        match s.to_lowercase().as_str() {\n            \"noaa-hrrr-bdp-pds\" | \"hrrr\" => Ok(S3Bucket::HrrrBdp),\n            \"noaa-gefs-pds\" | \"gefs\" => Ok(S3Bucket::GefsPds),\n            \"noaa-nbm-grib2-pds\" | \"nbm\" => Ok(S3Bucket::NbmGrib2),\n            \"noaa-gfs-bdp-pds\" | \"gfs\" => Ok(S3Bucket::GfsPds),\n            _ => Err(FetchError::InvalidUrl(format!(\"Unknown S3 bucket: {}\", s))),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Google Cloud Storage buckets mirroring NOAA data\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\npub enum GcsBucket {\n    /// HRRR model data on GCS\n    HighResolutionRapidRefresh,\n    /// NBM model data on GCS\n    NationalBlendOfModels,\n    /// GEFS model data on GCS\n    GfsEnsembleForecastSystem,\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for GcsBucket {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            GcsBucket::HighResolutionRapidRefresh => write!(f, \"high-resolution-rapid-refresh\"),\n            GcsBucket::NationalBlendOfModels => write!(f, \"national-blend-of-models\"),\n            GcsBucket::GfsEnsembleForecastSystem => write!(f, \"gfs-ensemble-forecast-system\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl GcsBucket {\n    /// Get the full GCS bucket URL\n    pub fn base_url(&self) -> String {\n        format!(\"{}{b}/\", GCS_BASE, b = self)\n    }\n\n    /// Convert from string\n    pub fn from_str(s: &str) -> Result<Self> {\n        match s.to_lowercase().as_str() {\n            \"high-resolution-rapid-refresh\" | \"hrrr\" => Ok(GcsBucket::HighResolutionRapidRefresh),\n            \"national-blend-of-models\" | \"nbm\" => Ok(GcsBucket::NationalBlendOfModels),\n            \"gfs-ensemble-forecast-system\" | \"gefs\" => Ok(GcsBucket::GfsEnsembleForecastSystem),\n            _ => Err(FetchError::InvalidUrl(format!(\"Unknown GCS bucket: {}\", s))),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// NOMADS model endpoints\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\npub enum NomadsModel {\n    /// GFS model via NOMADS\n    Gfs,\n    /// GEFS model via NOMADS\n    Gefs,\n    /// NAM model via NOMADS\n    Nam,\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for NomadsModel {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            NomadsModel::Gfs => write!(f, \"gfs\"),\n            NomadsModel::Gefs => write!(f, \"gefs\"),\n            NomadsModel::Nam => write!(f, \"nam\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl NomadsModel {\n    /// Get the base NOMADS URL for this model\n    pub fn base_url(&self) -> String {\n        format!(\"{}{m}/\", NOMADS_BASE, m = self)\n    }\n\n    /// Convert from string\n    pub fn from_str(s: &str) -> Result<Self> {\n        match s.to_lowercase().as_str() {\n            \"gfs\" => Ok(NomadsModel::Gfs),\n            \"gefs\" => Ok(NomadsModel::Gefs),\n            \"nam\" => Ok(NomadsModel::Nam),\n            _ => Err(FetchError::InvalidUrl(format!(\"Unknown NOMADS model: {}\", s))),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl DataProvider {\n    /// Get the base URL for this provider\n    pub fn base_url(&self) -> String {\n        match self {\n            DataProvider::S3(bucket) => bucket.base_url(),\n            DataProvider::Gcs(bucket) => bucket.base_url(),\n            DataProvider::Nomads(model) => {"type":"assistant","message":{"id":"msg_20260702200953a1b590c873d94e6b","type":"message","role":"assistant","model":"glm-4.7","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"call_344a6b5ea35c4935b6148b49","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/home/coding/gribtract/crates/gribtract-fetch/src/client.rs","content":"//! HTTP client for byte-range fetching\n\nuse crate::error::{FetchError, Result};\nuse crate::provider::{DataProvider, StorageProvider};\nuse bytes::Bytes;\nuse std::ops::Range;\nuse std::time::Duration;\n\n/// HTTP range request specification\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct RangeRequest {\n    /// Start byte position (inclusive)\n    pub start: u64,\n    /// End byte position (inclusive), or None for fetch to end\n    pub end: Option<u64>,\n}\n\nimpl RangeRequest {\n    /// Create a new range request\n    pub fn new(start: u64, end: Option<u64>) -> Self {\n        Self { start, end }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a range request with a specified length\n    pub fn with_length(start: u64, length: u64) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            start,\n            end: Some(start + length - 1),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a range request from a Rust Range (end is exclusive)\n    pub fn from_range(range: Range<u64>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            start: range.start,\n            end: if range.end > 0 {\n                Some(range.end - 1)\n            } else {\n                None\n            },\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the Range header value for HTTP requests\n    pub fn to_header_value(&self) -> String {\n        match self.end {\n            Some(end) => format!(\"bytes={}-{}\", self.start, end),\n            None => format!(\"bytes={}-\", self.start),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the total number of bytes requested\n    pub fn length(&self) -> Option<u64> {\n        self.end.map(|end| end - self.start + 1)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Response from a range request\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub struct RangeResponse {\n    /// The requested bytes\n    pub data: Bytes,\n    /// The actual content range returned by the server\n    pub content_range: ContentRange,\n    /// Total size of the resource (if known)\n    pub total_size: Option<u64>,\n}\n\n/// Content-Range header information\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ContentRange {\n    /// Start byte position\n    pub start: u64,\n    /// End byte position (inclusive)\n    pub end: u64,\n    /// Total resource size (or None if unknown/asterisk)\n    pub total: Option<u64>,\n}\n\nimpl ContentRange {\n    /// Parse from Content-Range header value\n    ///\n    /// Format: `bytes start-end/total` or `bytes */total` or `bytes start-end/*`\n    pub fn parse(header: &str) -> Result<Self> {\n        let header = header.trim();\n        if !header.starts_with(\"bytes \") {\n            return Err(FetchError::InvalidContentRange(format!(\n                \"Missing 'bytes' prefix: {}\",\n                header\n            )));\n        }\n\n        let parts: Vec<&str> = header[6..].split('/').collect();\n        if parts.len() != 2 {\n            return Err(FetchError::InvalidContentRange(format!(\n                \"Invalid format: {}\",\n                header\n            )));\n        }\n\n        let total = match parts[1] {\n            \"*\" => None,\n            s => Some(\n                s.parse::<u64>()\n                    .map_err(|_| FetchError::InvalidContentRange(format!(\"Invalid total: {}\", s)))?,\n            ),\n        };\n\n        if parts[0] == \"*\" {\n            return Ok(ContentRange {\n                start: 0,\n                end: 0,\n                total,\n            });\n        }\n\n        let range_parts: Vec<&str> = parts[0].split('-').collect();\n        if range_parts.len() != 2 {\n            return Err(FetchError::InvalidContentRange(format!(\n                \"Invalid range format: {}\",\n                header\n            )));\n        }\n\n        let start = range_parts[0]\n            .parse::<u64>()\n            .map_err(|_| FetchError::InvalidContentRange(format!(\"Invalid start: {}\", range_parts[0])))?;\n        let end = range_parts[1]\n            .parse::<u64>()\n            .map_err(|_| FetchError::InvalidContentRange(format!(\"Invalid end: {}\", range_parts[1])))?;\n\n        Ok(ContentRange { start, end, total })\n    }\n\n    /// Get the length of this range\n    pub fn length(&self) -> u64 {\n        self.end - self.start + 1\n    }\n}\n\n/// HTTP client for fetching GRIB2 data via byte-range requests\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct FetchClient {\n    client: reqwest::Client,\n    default_timeout: Duration,\n}\n\nimpl FetchClient {\n    /// Create a new fetch client with default settings\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self::with_timeout(Duration::from_secs(30))\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new fetch client with a specified timeout\n    pub fn with_timeout(timeout: Duration) -> Self {\n        let client = reqwest::Client::builder()\n            .timeout(timeout)\n            .gzip(true)\n            .build()\n            .expect(\"Failed to build HTTP client\");\n\n        Self {\n            client,\n            default_timeout: timeout,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a fetch client from an existing reqwest client\n    pub fn from_client(client: reqwest::Client) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            default_timeout: Duration::from_secs(30),\n            client,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Fetch a byte range from a URL\n    pub async fn fetch_range(&self, url: &str, range: RangeRequest) -> Result<RangeResponse> {\n        let header_value = range.to_header_value();\n\n        let response = self\n            .client\n            .get(url)\n            .header(\"Range\", header_value)\n            .send()\n            .await?;\n\n        let status = response.status();\n\n        if !status.is_success() {\n            return Err(FetchError::HttpStatus(status));\n        }\n\n        let content_range_header = response\n            .headers()\n            .get(\"content-range\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok());\n\n        let content_range = match content_range_header {\n            Some(header) => ContentRange::parse(header)?,\n            None => {\n                // Some providers might not return Content-Range for 200 OK responses\n                // (they return the full resource instead of a range)\n                return Err(FetchError::InvalidContentRange(\n                    \"Missing Content-Range header\".into(),\n                ));\n            }\n        };\n\n        let total_size = content_range.total;\n        let data = response.bytes().await?;\n\n        Ok(RangeResponse {\n            data,\n            content_range,\n            total_size,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Fetch the first N bytes from a URL\n    pub async fn fetch_head(&self, url: &str, length: u64) -> Result<RangeResponse> {\n        self.fetch_range(url, RangeRequest::with_length(0, length)).await\n    }\n\n    /// Fetch the entire resource (no range request)\n    pub async fn fetch_all(&self, url: &str) -> Result<Bytes> {\n        let response = self.client.get(url).send().await?;\n\n        let status = response.status();\n        if !status.is_success() {\n            return Err(FetchError::HttpStatus(status));\n        }\n\n        Ok(response.bytes().await?)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the resource size with a HEAD request (Content-Length header)\n    pub async fn resource_size(&self, url: &str) -> Result<Option<u64>> {\n        let response = self.client.head(url).send().await?;\n        let status = response.status();\n\n        if !status.is_success() {\n            return Err(FetchError::HttpStatus(status));\n        }\n\n        Ok(response\n            .headers()\n            .get(\"content-length\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n            .and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok()))\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the URL is accessible and supports range requests\n    pub async fn probe(&self, url: &str) -> Result<ProbeInfo> {\n        let start = std::time::Instant::now();\n\n        let response = self\n            .client\n            .head(url)\n            .send()\n            .await?;\n\n        let status = response.status();\n        if !status.is_success() {\n            return Err(FetchError::HttpStatus(status));\n        }\n\n        let connect_time = start.elapsed();\n        let supports_range = response\n            .headers()\n            .get(\"accept-ranges\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n            .map(|v| v.to_lowercase() == \"bytes\")\n            .unwrap_or(false);\n\n        let content_length = response\n            .headers()\n            .get(\"content-length\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n            .and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok());\n\n        Ok(ProbeInfo {\n            url: url.to_string(),\n            connect_time,\n            supports_range,\n            content_length,\n        })\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for FetchClient {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Information about a URL from a probe request\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ProbeInfo {\n    /// The URL that was probed\n    pub url: String,\n    /// Time to establish connection and get HEAD response\n    pub connect_time: Duration,\n    /// Whether the server supports range requests\n    pub supports_range: bool,\n    /// Content length if available\n    pub content_length: Option<u64>,\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_range_request_header() {\n        let range = RangeRequest::new(100, Some(199));\n        assert_eq!(range.to_header_value(), \"bytes=100-199\");\n\n        let range = RangeRequest::new(100, None);\n        assert_eq!(range.to_header_value(), \"bytes=100-\");\n\n        let range = RangeRequest::with_length(0, 1024);\n        assert_eq!(range.to_header_value(), \"bytes=0-1023\");\n\n        let range = RangeRequest::from_range(100..200);\n        assert_eq!(range.to_header_value(), \"bytes=100-199\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_content_range_parse() {\n        let cr = ContentRange::parse(\"bytes 0-1023/2048\").unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(cr.start, 0);\n        assert_eq!(cr.end, 1023);\n        assert_eq!(cr.total, Some(2048));\n        assert_eq!(cr.length(), 1024);\n\n        let cr = ContentRange::parse(\"bytes 0-1023/*\").unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(cr.start, 0);\n        assert_eq!(cr.end, 1023);\n        assert_eq!(cr.total, None);\n\n        let cr = ContentRange::parse(\"bytes */2048\").unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(cr.start, 0);\n        assert_eq!(cr.end, 0);\n        assert_eq!(cr.total, Some(2048));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_provider_urls() {\n        use crate::provider::{S3Bucket, GcsBucket, NomadsModel, StorageProvider};\n\n        let hrrr = S3Bucket::HrrrBdp;\n        assert_eq!(\n            hrrr.base_url(),\n            \"https://noaa-noaa-hrrr-bdp-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/\"\n        );\n\n        let hrrr_gcs = GcsBucket::HighResolutionRapidRefresh;\n        assert_eq!(\n            hrrr_gcs.base_url(),\n            \"https://storage.googleapis.com/high-resolution-rapid-refresh/\"\n        );\n\n        let gfs = NomadsModel::Gfs;\n        assert_eq!(gfs.base_url(), \"https://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/gfs/\");\n    }\n}\n"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":0},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"0af65f08-cd74-4ec3-b1bb-25a138e5e1cc","uuid":"81859f4d-5b42-4419-b026-fd770e36c1cf"}

std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::time::Duration;\n\n/// Result of probing a single provider for a specific model\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ProbeResult {\n    /// The provider that was probed\n    pub provider: String,\n    /// URL used for probing (typically a recent .idx file)\n    pub probe_url: String,\n    /// TCP connect time + TTFB for the probe request\n    pub connect_ms: u64,\n    /// Time to first byte (TTFB) in milliseconds\n    pub ttfb_ms: u64,\n    /// Throughput measured during the probe (bytes/sec)\n    pub throughput_mbs: f64,\n    /// Combined score (lower is better)\n    pub score: f64,\n    /// Whether the probe was successful\n    pub success: bool,\n    /// Error message if the probe failed\n    pub error: Option<String>,\n    /// Timestamp of the probe\n    pub timestamp: String,\n}\n\n/// Provider probe results for all providers and models\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ProviderProbeResults {\n    /// Results per model\n    pub models: HashMap<String, Vec<ProbeResult>>,\n    /// Timestamp when the probe was run\n    pub timestamp: String,\n    /// Git commit hash for reproducibility\n    pub git_sha: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Provider probe client\n///\n/// Probes each provider for each model at startup to determine the fastest provider\n/// for the current deployment location.\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub struct ProviderProbe {\n    client: FetchClient,\n    /// Test files to use for probing (small .idx files)\n    probe_files: HashMap<String, Vec<(String, String)>>,\n}\n\nimpl ProviderProbe {\n    /// Create a new provider probe client\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            client: FetchClient::new(),\n            probe_files: Self::default_probe_files(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new provider probe client with a custom HTTP client\n    pub fn with_client(client: FetchClient) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            client,\n            probe_files: Self::default_probe_files(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get default probe files for each model\n    ///\n    /// Returns a map of model name to list of (provider, url) tuples\n    fn default_probe_files() -> HashMap<String, Vec<(String, String)>> {\n        let mut files = HashMap::new();\n\n        // HRRR model probe files\n        files.insert(\n            \"hrrr\".to_string(),\n            vec![\n                (\"s3:hrrr-bdp\".to_string(), \"https://noaa-hrrr-bdp-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/hrrr.20250702/conus/hrrr.t00z.wrfsfcf00.grib2.idx\".to_string()),\n                (\"gcs:hrrr\".to_string(), \"https://storage.googleapis.com/high-resolution-rapid-refresh/hrrr.20250702/conus/hrrr.t00z.wrfsfcf00.grib2.idx\".to_string()),\n            ],\n        );\n\n        // GEFS model probe files\n        files.insert(\n            \"gefs\".to_string(),\n            vec![\n                (\"s3:gefs-pds\".to_string(), \"https://noaa-gefs-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/gefs.20250702/00/wave/grb2/gefs.wave.t00z.prtcgrb.idx.0p25\".to_string()),\n                (\"gcs:gefs\".to_string(), \"https://storage.googleapis.com/gfs-ensemble-forecast-system/gefs.20250702/00/wave/grb2/gefs.wave.t00z.prtcgrb.idx.0p25\".to_string()),\n            ],\n        );\n\n        // NBM model probe files\n        files.insert(\n            \"nbm\".to_string(),\n            vec![\n                (\"s3:nbm-grib2\".to_string(), \"https://noaa-nbm-grib2-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/nbm.20250702/nbm.t00z.conusnest.2p5.f000.co.grib2.idx\".to_string()),\n                (\"gcs:nbm\".to_string(), \"https://storage.googleapis.com/national-blend-of-models/nbm.20250702/nbm.t00z.conusnest.2p5.f000.co.grib2.idx\".to_string()),\n            ],\n        );\n\n        // GFS model probe files\n        files.insert(\n            \"gfs\".to_string(),\n            vec![\n                (\"s3:gfs-pds\".to_string(), \"https://noaa-gfs-bdp-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/gfs.20250702/00/gfs.t00z.pgrb2.0p25.f000.idx\".to_string()),\n                (\"nomads:gfs\".to_string(), \"https://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/pub/data/nccf/com/gfs/prod/gfs.20250702/00/atmos/gfs.t00z.pgrb2.0p25.f000.idx\".to_string()),\n            ],\n        );\n\n        files\n    }\n\n    /// Probe all providers for all models\n    pub async fn probe_all(&self) -> ProviderProbeResults {\n        let mut results = HashMap::new();\n        let timestamp = chrono::Utc::now().to_rfc3339();\n\n        for (model, probe_files) in &self.probe_files {\n            let mut model_results = Vec::new();\n\n            for (provider, url) in probe_files {\n                let result = self.probe_url(provider, url).await;\n                model_results.push(result);\n            }\n\n            // Sort by score (lower is better)\n            model_results.sort_by(|a, b| {\n                a.score.partial_cmp(&b.score).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal)\n            });\n\n            results.insert(model.clone(), model_results);\n        }\n\n        ProviderProbeResults {\n            models: results,\n            timestamp,\n            git_sha: Self::get_git_sha(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Probe a single URL\n    async fn probe_url(&self, provider: &str, url: &str) -> ProbeResult {\n        let start = std::time::Instant::now();\n\n        match self.probe_url_inner(url).await {\n            Ok(result) => {\n                let elapsed = start.elapsed();\n                ProbeResult {\n                    provider: provider.to_string(),\n                    probe_url: url.to_string(),\n                    connect_ms: result.connect_time.as_millis() as u64,\n                    ttfb_ms: result.ttfb.as_millis() as u64,\n                    throughput_mbs: result.throughput_mbs,\n                    score: Self::calculate_score(&result),\n                    success: true,\n                    error: None,\n                    timestamp: chrono::Utc::now().to_rfc3339(),\n                }\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                let elapsed = start.elapsed();\n                ProbeResult {\n                    provider: provider.to_string(),\n                    probe_url: url.to_string(),\n                    connect_ms: elapsed.as_millis() as u64,\n                    ttfb_ms: elapsed.as_millis() as u64,\n                    throughput_mbs: 0.0,\n                    score: f64::MAX,\n                    success: false,\n                    error: Some(e.to_string()),\n                    timestamp: chrono::Utc::now().to_rfc3339(),\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Inner probe logic for a single URL\n    async fn probe_url_inner(&self, url: &str) -> Result<ProbeInnerResult, crate::FetchError> {\n        let start = std::time::Instant::now();\n\n        // First, do a HEAD request to measure connect time and check range support\n        let head_response = self.client.client.head(url).send().await?;\n        let connect_time = start.elapsed();\n\n        let status = head_response.status();\n        if !status.is_success() {\n            return Err(crate::FetchError::HttpStatus(status));\n        }\n\n        // Check if range requests are supported\n        let supports_range = head_response\n            .headers()\n            .get(\"accept-ranges\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n            .map(|v| v.to_lowercase() == \"bytes\")\n            .unwrap_or(false);\n\n        // Get content length\n        let content_length = head_response\n            .headers()\n            .get(\"content-length\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n            .and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok())\n            .unwrap_or(0);\n\n        // Now do a small range request to measure TTFB and throughput\n        let range_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n\n        let range_response = if supports_range && content_length > 1024 {\n            Some(self.client.fetch_head(url, 1024).await?)\n        } else {\n            None\n        };\n\n        let ttfb = range_start.elapsed();\n\n        let throughput_mbs = if let Some(resp) = range_response {\n            let bytes = resp.data.len() as f64;\n            let seconds = ttfb.as_secs_f64();\n            (bytes / seconds) / (1024.0 * 1024.0)\n        } else {\n            0.0\n        };\n\n        Ok(ProbeInnerResult {\n            connect_time,\n            ttfb,\n            throughput_mbs,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Calculate a combined score for provider selection\n    ///\n    /// Lower is better. Formula: connect_ms + ttfb_ms + (1 / throughput_mbs)\n    fn calculate_score(result: &ProbeInnerResult) -> f64 {\n        let connect_penalty = result.connect_time.as_millis() as f64;\n        let ttfb_penalty = result.ttfb.as_millis() as f64;\n        let throughput_penalty = if result.throughput_mbs > 0.0 {\n            1000.0 / result.throughput_mbs\n        } else {\n            10000.0 // Large penalty for no throughput\n        };\n\n        connect_penalty + ttfb_penalty + throughput_penalty\n    }\n\n    /// Get the current git SHA for reproducibility\n    fn get_git_sha() -> Option<String> {\n        std::process::Command::new(\"git\")\n            .args([\"rev-parse\", \"HEAD\"])\n            .output()\n            .ok()\n            .and_then(|output| {\n                let sha = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout).trim().to_string();\n                if !sha.is_empty() {\n                    Some(sha)\n                } else {\n                    None\n                }\n            })\n    }\n\n    /// Get the best provider for a model based on probe results\n    pub fn get_best_provider(\n        results: &ProviderProbeResults,\n        model: &str,\n    ) -> Option<&ProbeResult> {\n        results.models.get(model).and_then(|model_results| {\n            model_results.iter().find(|r| r.success)\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Write probe results to a JSON file\n    pub fn write_results(results: &ProviderProbeResults, path: &std::path::Path) -> std::io::Result<()> {\n        let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(results)?;\n        std::fs::write(path, json)\n    }\n\n    /// Load probe results from a JSON file\n    pub fn load_results(path: &std::path::Path) -> std::io::Result<ProviderProbeResults> {\n        let json = std::fs::read_to_string(path)?;\n        let results: ProviderProbeResults = serde_json::from_str(&json)?;\n        Ok(results)\n    }\n\n    /// Check if probe results are stale (older than 24 hours)\n    pub fn is_stale(results: &ProviderProbeResults, max_age: Duration) -> bool {\n        match chrono::DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339(&results.timestamp) {\n            Ok(timestamp) => {\n                let now = chrono::Utc::now();\n                let age = now.signed_duration_since(timestamp);\n                age.to_std().unwrap_or(Duration::ZERO) > max_age\n            }\n            Err(_) => true, // Invalid timestamp means stale\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for ProviderProbe {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Inner result from a single probe\n#[derive(Debug)]\nstruct ProbeInnerResult {\n    connect_time: Duration,\n    ttfb: Duration,\n    throughput_mbs: f64,\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    #[ignore] // Requires network access\n    async fn test_probe_hrrr() {\n        let probe = ProviderProbe::new();\n        let results = probe.probe_all().await;\n\n        println!(\"Probe results: {}\", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&results).unwrap());\n\n        // Check that we got results for HRRR\n        assert!(results.models.contains_key(\"hrrr\"));\n\n        let hrrr_results = &results.models[\"hrrr\"];\n        assert!(!hrrr_results.is_empty());\n\n        // At least one provider should succeed\n        let successful = hrrr_results.iter().filter(|r| r.success).count();\n        assert!(successful > 0, \"At least one HRRR provider should succeed\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_calculation() {\n        let result = ProbeInnerResult {\n            connect_time: Duration::from_millis(50),\n            ttfb: Duration::from_millis(100),\n            throughput_mbs: 10.0,\n        };\n\n        let score = ProviderProbe::calculate_score(&result);\n        // connect_penalty = 50, ttfb_penalty = 100, throughput_penalty = 1000/10 = 100\n        // total = 250\n        assert!((score - 250.0).abs() < 1.0);\n    }\n}\n"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":0},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"0af65f08-cd74-4ec3-b1bb-25a138e5e1cc","uuid":"1a6be456-a0ad-4cd9-8d4d-658cef0c0fae"}

supports_range = head_response\n            .headers()\n            .get(\"accept-ranges\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n            .map(|v| v.to_lowercase() == \"bytes\")\n v.to_str().ok())\n            .and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok())\n            .unwrap_or(0);\n\n        // Now do a small range request to measure TTFB and throughput\n        let range_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n\n        let range_response = if supports_range && content_length > 1024 {\n            Some(self.client.fetch_head(url, 1024).await?)\n        } else {\n            None\n        };\n\n        let ttfb = range_start.elapsed();\n\n        let throughput_mbs = if let Some(resp) = range_response {\n            let bytes = resp.data.len() as f64;\n            let seconds = ttfb.as_secs_f64();\n            (bytes / seconds) / (1024.0 * 1024.0)\n        } else {\n            0.0\n        };\n\n        Ok(ProbeInnerResult {\n            connect_time,\n            ttfb,\n            throughput_mbs,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Calculate a combined score for provider selection\n    ///\n    /// Lower is better. Formula: connect_ms + ttfb_ms + (1 / throughput_mbs)\n    fn calculate_score(result: &ProbeInnerResult) -> f64 {\n        let connect_penalty = result.connect_time.as_millis() as f64;\n        let ttfb_penalty = result.ttfb.as_millis() as f64;\n        let throughput_penalty = if result.throughput_mbs > 0.0 {\n            1000.0 / result.throughput_mbs\n        } else {\n            10000.0 // Large penalty for no throughput\n        };\n\n        connect_penalty + ttfb_penalty + throughput_penalty\n    }\n\n    /// Get the current git SHA for reproducibility\n    fn get_git_sha() -> Option<String> {\n        std::process::Command::new(\"git\")\n            .args([\"rev-parse\", \"HEAD\"])\n            .output()\n            .ok()\n            .and_then(|output| {\n                let sha = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout).trim().to_string();\n                if !sha.is_empty() {\n                    Some(sha)\n                } else {\n                    None\n                }\n            })\n    }\n\n    /// Get the best provider for a model based on probe results\n    pub fn get_best_provider(\n        results: &ProviderProbeResults,\n        model: &str,\n    ) -> Option<&ProbeResult> {\n        results.models.get(model).and_then(|model_results| {\n            model_results.iter().find(|r| r.success)\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Write probe results to a JSON file\n    pub fn write_results(results: &ProviderProbeResults, path: &std::path::Path) -> std::io::Result<()> {\n        let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(results)?;\n        std::fs::write(path, json)\n    }\n\n    /// Load probe results from a JSON file\n    pub fn load_results(path: &std::path::Path) -> std::io::Result<ProviderProbeResults> {\n        let json = std::fs::read_to_string(path)?;\n        let results: ProviderProbeResults = serde_json::from_str(&json)?;\n        Ok(results)\n    }\n\n    /// Check if probe results are stale (older than 24 hours)\n    pub fn is_stale(results: &ProviderProbeResults, max_age: Duration) -> bool {\n        match chrono::DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339(&results.timestamp) {\n            Ok(timestamp) => {\n                let now = chrono::Utc::now();\n                let age = now.signed_duration_since(timestamp);\n                age.to_std().unwrap_or(Duration::ZERO) > max_age\n            }\n            Err(_) => true, // Invalid timestamp means stale\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for ProviderProbe {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Inner result from a single probe\n#[derive(Debug)]\nstruct ProbeInnerResult {\n    connect_time: Duration,\n    ttfb: Duration,\n    throughput_mbs: f64,\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    #[ignore] // Requires network access\n    async fn test_probe_hrrr() {\n        let probe = ProviderProbe::new();\n        let results = probe.probe_all().await;\n\n        println!(\"Probe results: {}\", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&results).unwrap());\n\n        // Check that we got results for HRRR\n        assert!(results.models.contains_key(\"hrrr\"));\n\n        let hrrr_results = &results.models[\"hrrr\"];\n        assert!(!hrrr_results.is_empty());\n\n        // At least one provider should succeed\n        let successful = hrrr_results.iter().filter(|r| r.success).count();\n        assert!(successful > 0, \"At least one HRRR provider should succeed\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_calculation() {\n        let result = ProbeInnerResult {\n            connect_time: Duration::from_millis(50),\n            ttfb: Duration::from_millis(100),\n            throughput_mbs: 10.0,\n        };\n\n        let score = ProviderProbe::calculate_score(&result);\n        // connect_penalty = 50, ttfb_penalty = 100, throughput_penalty = 1000/10 = 100\n        // total = 250\n        assert!((score - 250.0).abs() < 1.0);\n    }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null,"userModified":false}}

gribtract-fetch\n\nHTTP byte-range fetching for NOAA GRIB2 data from S3, GCS, and NOMADS.\n\n## Overview\n\nThis crate provides HTTP byte-range request support for retrieving GRIB2 files from NOAA's public data sources:\n\n- **NOAA S3 buckets**: `noaa-hrrr-bdp-pds`, `noaa-gefs-pds`, `noaa-nbm-grib2-pds`, `noaa-gfs-bdp-pds`\n- **Google Cloud Storage**: `high-resolution-rapid-refresh`, `national-blend-of-models`, `gfs-ensemble-forecast-system`\n- **NOMADS**: `nomads.ncep.noaa.gov`\n\nByte-range requests allow efficient retrieval of specific GRIB2 messages within a file without downloading the entire file.\n\n## Features\n\n- `async`: Enable async HTTP client with tokio support\n- `probe`: Enable provider probing and selection\n\n## Example\n\n```rust\nuse gribtract_fetch::{FetchClient, RangeRequest};\n\n#[tokio::main]\nasync fn main() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {\n    let client = FetchClient::new();\n\n    // Fetch a 1KB range from a GRIB2 file\n    let range = RangeRequest::with_length(0, 1024);\n    let url = \"https://noaa-hrrr-bdp-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/hrrr.20250702/conus/hrrr.t00z.wrfsfcf00.grib2\";\n\n    let response = client.fetch_range(url, range).await?;\n    println!(\"Fetched {} bytes\", response.data.len());\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n```\n\n## Provider Probing\n\nThe `probe` feature enables automatic provider selection based on latency and throughput:\n\n```rust\nuse gribtract_fetch::probe::ProviderProbe;\n\n#[tokio::main]\nasync fn main() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {\n    let probe = ProviderProbe::new();\n    let results = probe.probe_all().await;\n\n    // Get the best provider for HRRR\n    if let Some(best) = ProviderProbe::get_best_provider(&results, \"hrrr\") {\n        println!(\"Best HRRR provider: {} (score: {:.2})\", best.provider, best.score);\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n```\n\n## License\n\nMIT OR gribtract-fetch\n\nHTTP byte-range fetching for NOAA GRIB2 data from S3, GCS, and NOMADS.\n\n## Overview\n\nThis crate provides HTTP byte-range request support for retrieving GRIB2 files from NOAA's public data sources:\n\n- **NOAA S3 buckets**: `noaa-hrrr-bdp-pds`, `noaa-gefs-pds`, `noaa-nbm-grib2-pds`, `noaa-gfs-bdp-pds`\n- **Google Cloud Storage**: `high-resolution-rapid-refresh`, `national-blend-of-models`, `gfs-ensemble-forecast-system`\n- **NOMADS**: `nomads.ncep.noaa.gov`\n\nByte-range requests allow efficient retrieval of specific GRIB2 messages within a file without downloading the entire file.\n\n## Features\n\n- `async`: Enable async HTTP client with tokio support\n- `probe`: Enable provider probing and selection\n\n## Example\n\n```rust\nuse gribtract_fetch::{FetchClient, RangeRequest};\n\n#[tokio::main]\nasync fn main() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {\n    let client = FetchClient::new();\n\n    // Fetch a 1KB range from a GRIB2 file\n    let range = RangeRequest::with_length(0, 1024);\n    let url = \"https://noaa-hrrr-bdp-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/hrrr.20250702/conus/hrrr.t00z.wrfsfcf00.grib2\";\n\n    let response = client.fetch_range(url, range).await?;\n    println!(\"Fetched {} bytes\", response.data.len());\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n```\n\n## Provider Probing\n\nThe `probe` feature enables automatic provider selection based on latency and throughput:\n\n```rust\nuse gribtract_fetch::probe::ProviderProbe;\n\n#[tokio::main]\nasync fn main() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {\n    let probe = ProviderProbe::new();\n    let results = probe.probe_all().await;\n\n    // Get the best provider for HRRR\n    if let Some(best) = ProviderProbe::get_best_provider(&results, \"hrrr\") {\n        println!(\"Best HRRR provider: {} (score: {:.2})\", best.provider, best.score);\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n```\n\n## License\n\nMIT OR
